hero.headline: Begin Your New Chapter
hero.subheading: A safe, structured, and supportive community for men committed to lasting recovery. Walk beside the river. Rebuild your life.

who_we_are.heading: Recovery built on community & purpose
who_we_are.body: River Sober Living is a men's sober living community in Boise, Idaho, founded in 2017 with a simple belief: lasting recovery happens in the context of genuine community, personal accountability, and intentional structure. Situated along the Boise River Greenbelt, our homes offer more than a safe place to sleep. We offer a framework for growth — one that bridges the gap between residential treatment and independent living.

leap.heading: The LEAP Program
leap.subheading: LEAP is the foundation of life at River Sober Living — a four-pillar framework designed to guide each resident toward meaningful, sustainable recovery.

pillars.title: Living
pillars.description: Learning to live well in sobriety means establishing daily routines, healthy habits, and a stable home environment.

pillars.title: Engaging
pillars.description: Recovery doesn't happen in isolation. Residents engage with peers, house managers, and the broader recovery community.

pillars.title: Achieving
pillars.description: We support residents in setting and reaching meaningful goals — employment, education, financial independence.

pillars.title: Persevering
pillars.description: Long-term recovery requires resilience. We equip residents with coping tools and relapse-prevention strategies.

house_rules.heading: Structure & House Rules
requirements: Active participation in a 12-Step or recovery program
Attend a minimum of 3 meetings per week
Maintain employment, education, or active job search
Weekly house meetings and chores participation
Comply with all house curfews and schedules
Obtain and work with a sponsor
Random drug and alcohol testing at any time
Respect for all housemates and house property
expectations: No drugs, alcohol, or paraphernalia on property at any time
No guests without prior house manager approval
Maintain personal space and shared common areas
Pay rent on time and fulfill financial commitments
No disruptive, threatening, or abusive behavior
Support your fellow residents in their recovery
house_rules.zero_tolerance: River Sober Living maintains a strict zero tolerance policy for drug and alcohol use. Any resident found to be using substances will be asked to leave immediately.

houses.name: Kuna House
houses.description: Located in the peaceful community of Kuna, just outside Boise. A quiet, residential setting ideal for men seeking a calm environment.
houses.details: All LEAP program requirements apply. House manager on-site support.

houses.name: Avanti House
houses.description: Located in Boise with easy access to meetings, employment, and support services. A strong community house built on accountability.
houses.details: Full LEAP programming, regular drug testing, required meetings and house activities.

houses.name: Network House
houses.description: A well-established Boise home focused on building connections to recovery, community, and a new way of life.
houses.details: Structured daily schedule, peer accountability, and dedicated house manager oversight.

houses.name: McMullin House
houses.description: One of our established Boise locations offering a stable, community-oriented environment close to the Greenbelt.
houses.details: Structured daily schedule, peer accountability, and dedicated house manager oversight.

houses.name: Tyson House
houses.description: A strong, brotherhood-focused environment where men hold each other accountable and grow together.
houses.details: All standard LEAP program requirements, zero tolerance policy in full effect.

testimonials.quote: River gave me more than a place to sleep. It gave me brothers. The structure was hard at first, but it was exactly what I needed.
I came here straight from treatment. The guys at River showed me what a sober life actually looks like. I have a job, an apartment, and my daughter back.
The LEAP program isn't just a name. They actually walk you through it. This program changed my trajectory entirely.
The transition from inpatient to real life can be terrifying. River made it manageable. Safe. Even hopeful.
testimonials.author_detail: Hazelden Betty Ford Referral • Tyson House

testimonials.quote: I relapsed twice before coming to River. I'm 14 months clean and counting.

faq.question: Who is eligible to live at River Sober Living?
faq.answer: River Sober Living is for adult men (18+) who are committed to sobriety and seeking a structured, supportive environment.

faq.question: How long can I live at a River Sober Living house?
faq.answer: We have no fixed maximum stay — residents remain as long as they are actively working their recovery and meeting house requirements.

faq.question: What does rent include?
faq.answer: Rent covers a furnished room, utilities, Wi-Fi, and access to all LEAP program resources and house manager support.

faq.question: Do you accept referrals from treatment centers?
faq.answer: Yes. We are a Preferred Provider through Hazelden Betty Ford Foundation and work with referrals throughout Idaho and the surrounding region.

faq.question: What happens if a resident relapses?
faq.answer: Any resident found using drugs or alcohol will be asked to leave. We can assist departing residents in connecting with higher levels of care.

faq.question: How do I apply or check availability?
faq.answer: Call us at (208) 576-9527 or email marcus@riversoberliving.com. Our hours are Monday through Sunday, 8am–10pm.

faq.question: Are meetings required?
faq.answer: Yes. All residents must attend a minimum of 3 recovery meetings per week and have an active sponsor.
